content.text:
# Useful Links for "SQL Find Duplicate Values"

Here are some helpful resources for finding duplicate values in SQL:

## Official Documentation
- **PostgreSQL Docs** – [GROUP BY and HAVING Clauses](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/tutorial-agg.html)
- **MySQL Docs** – [GROUP BY Optimization](https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.0/en/group-by-optimization.html)
- **Microsoft SQL Server** – [GROUP BY (Transact-SQL)](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/queries/select-group-by-transact-sql)
- **SQLite Docs** – [Aggregate Functions](https://www.sqlite.org/lang_aggfunc.html)

## Tutorials & Guides
- **W3Schools** – [SQL GROUP BY Statement](https://www.w3schools.com/sql/sql_groupby.asp)
- **GeeksforGeeks** – [How to Find Duplicate Values in SQL](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/sql-query-to-find-duplicate-names-in-a-table/)
- **SQLShack** – [Different Ways to SQL Delete Duplicate Rows](https://www.sqlshack.com/different-ways-to-sql-delete-duplicate-rows-from-a-sql-table/)
- **Mode Analytics** – [SQL GROUP BY Tutorial](https://mode.com/sql-tutorial/sql-group-by/)

## Community Q&A
- **Stack Overflow** – [Find duplicate records in a table](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/2594829/finding-duplicate-values-in-a-sql-table)
- **Stack Overflow** – [How to find duplicate rows with SQL](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/18932/how-can-i-remove-duplicate-rows)

## Key Concepts to Search
- `GROUP BY` + `HAVING COUNT(*) > 1`
- `ROW_NUMBER()` window function with `PARTITION BY`
- `DISTINCT` keyword
- Common Table Expressions (CTEs) for deduplication

---

**Quick Tip:** The most common approach is:
```sql
SELECT column_name, COUNT(*)
FROM table_name
GROUP BY column_name
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
```

Let me know if you'd like help with a specific database system or scenario!
